dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3299 IS: Delivering Support for Hospitals in Tennessee Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Blackburn (for herself and Mr. Hagerty) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend title XIX of the Social Security Act to provide a permanent disproportionate share hospital allotment to Tennessee for fiscal year 2026 and succeeding fiscal years, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Delivering Support for Hospitals in Tennessee Act or the DSH in Tennessee Act.

section.header: Permanent Tennessee DSH allotment for fiscal year 2026 and succeeding fiscal years
section.text: Section 1923(f)(3) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396r–4(f)(3)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (A), by striking subparagraphs (E) and (F) and inserting subparagraphs (E), (F), and (G); and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

clause.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, the State of Tennessee shall receive a DSH allotment under this paragraph beginning with fiscal year 2026 and for each succeeding fiscal year.

Notwithstanding the table set forth in paragraph (2) or the terms of the TennCare Demonstration Project in effect for the State, the DSH allotment for Tennessee for fiscal year 2026, shall be equal to the DSH allotment for Tennessee under paragraph (6)(A)(vi) for fiscal year 2015, increased in each succeeding fiscal year by the percentage change in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (all items; U.S. city average).

clause.header: Treatment as a low DSH state
clause.text: For any fiscal year after fiscal year 2026—

subclause.text: Tennessee shall be deemed to be a State described in paragraph (5)(B); and

the DSH allotment for Tennessee shall be the DSH allotment for the previous fiscal year increased in the same manner as DSH allotments for low DSH States are increased for such fiscal year under clause (iii) of such paragraph.
